title: Tulvaree
content:

'''Tulvarees''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] [[Bird/Legends|avian]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Pochi]]. Each of their [[Limb/Legends|wings]] was tipped with three razor-sharp talons, and their small [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]] were filled with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. There were a number of different Tulvaree tribes, which engaged in intense warfare with each other. When the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Faarl]] the Conqueror took control of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the Tulvarees quickly learned how to take orders from the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]. Faarl, recognizing a source of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], provided a constant [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] supply to the Tulvarees in exchange for their allegiance.

==Biology and appearance==
The Tulvarees were [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] [[Bird/Legends|avians]]<ref name="CSWE III 267" /> who grew from 1.5 to 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height. Each member of the [[Species/Legends|species]] had a small, flat body, with a whip-like tail and four long, wispy [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]. Both the arms and legs ended in digits capped by razor-sharp talons. The hands featured three fingers each, while the feet had four toes, three pointing forward and a fourth pointing back. Thin skin ran from the joint of each knee up along the sides of the body and the outside edge of each arm. A series of [[Bone/Legends|bony]] struts spread out from each elbow. That formed a membranous wing that allowed the Tulvarees to fly. Nevertheless, the Tulvarees could also move about on the ground, albeit at a slower speed than when airborne. The Tulvaree head was made up of a rounded skull and a short muzzle full of sharp [[Tooth/Legends|fangs]]. The mandible was flat and pointed. The Tulvarees hatched from [[Egg/Legends|eggs]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Society and culture==
The Tulvarees lived in the [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] regions of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Pochi]], where they [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] grazing [[Diet/Legends|herbivores]] in groups. They were divided into large groups whose members were all part of a single extended family, also referred to as a tribe. Those groups lived together in caverns and other naturally occurring shelters. They laid their eggs in special [[hatching caves]], where the eggs were kept until the young emerged.<ref name="WBC" />

Combat was also a group vocation. Tribes considered all other Tulvaree groups their competitors: enemies to be attacked on sight. Due to the species' natural talons and fangs, Tulvaree wars could be quite [[Death/Legends|deadly]]. The only instance in which Tulvarees from different tribes would ignore their animosity was in the face of a mutual threat.<ref name="WBC" />

The species was considered only semi-sentient, possessing no obvious art, construction, [[Music/Legends|music]], or tools of indigenous design. Nevertheless, the Tulvarees were capable of learning foreign [[Language/Legends|languages]] and of following orders from a master. After the arrival of offworlders on Pochi, the Tulvarees quickly learned to use the weapons and tools that were introduced to them. With such foreign materials, the Tulvarees reached a feudal state of technology during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="WBC" />

==History==
[[File:Faarl.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Faarl the Conqueror introduced the Tulvarees to advanced technology during the Galactic Civil War.]]
The Tulvarees evolved on Pochi,<ref name="CSWE III 267" /> a dry [[Planet/Legends|world]] of scrub-covered plains punctuated by stunted copses of [[Tree/Legends|trees]] and small mountains. The species developed a tribal society characterized by almost constant intertribal warfare.<ref name="WBC" />

At some point, [[Scout/Legends|explorers]] from afar charted Pochi, and the world became part of the [[Parthovian Cluster/Legends|Parthovian Cluster]] of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. During the Galactic Civil War, a teenage [[Human/Legends|Human]] who called himself [[Faarl]] the Conqueror reached the world, which still lay beyond the borders of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]]. Faarl brought with him a band of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] who were helping their [[Captain/Legends|captain]] to systematically conquer worlds in Wild Space in order to set up his own private fiefdom.<ref name="WBC" />

From the strangers, the Tulvarees learned to use tools and to fight with weapons. The offworlders gave the avians plenty of [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], medicines, and other items from afar. The Tulvarees also learned to understand the strangers' language and to follow Faarl's commands. They were eager to help him as long as he continued to keep their [[Stomach/Legends|bellies]] full.<ref name="CSWE III 267" /><ref name="WBC" />

At first, Faarl tried to force members of rival Tulvaree tribes to join together in [[Army/Legends|armies]], but the avians were unable to ignore their ages-old rivalries. Thereafter, Faarl only formed [[Regiment/Legends|regiments]] composed of members of the same tribe. The Tulvarees became Faarl's main fighting force, doing his bidding as [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] in exchange for food and offworld merchandise.<ref name="WBC" />

Meanwhile, Faarl decided to make his headquarters on Pochi in an abandoned tower he found there, which he dubbed [[Fortress Baarlos]]. There, he ordered large camps built for military training as well as camps constructed to house [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] laborers. The [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] also constructed the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Vindru]], which had its own [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. In the end, Pochi saw an influx of offworlders, either traders or slaves, but all lured to the world by Faarl.<ref name="WBC" /> He named the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] after himself: the [[Faarlsun system/Legends|Faarlsun system]].<ref name="EAOC 3">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>

The new power structure on Pochi lasted through the Galactic Civil War and into the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], when [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 50,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on Faarl's head.<ref name="WBC" /> The Tulvarees' service to Faarl came to an end when the Human [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] stormed Fortress Baarlos, bested the Tulvaree guards in combat, and seized Faarl to collect the New Republic bounty.<ref name="HOTM">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Creepy_bat2.png|thumb|right|upright|The alternate depiction provided by ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'']]
{{Quote|If you go out at night—Beware. I am there. Everywhere. Between the stars. Under the clouds. Watching you.|Unidentified member of this species|<ref name="MAGL" />}}
The Tulvarees are described in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a sourcebook written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published by [[West End Games]] in [[1993]]. The book presents statistics to allow players to portray a Tulvaree character; these make them significantly weaker than most other species since Tulvaree characters begin play with a smaller pool from which the player may distribute the character's attribute scores. Prosperi presents the Tulvarees as quite agile in comparison to Humans, yet slightly worse off in perception, and highly handicapped in intelligence, and aptitude for machines and other technical matters. The species receives bonuses for fighting with their talons and teeth.<ref name="WBC" /> The image used to illustrate the Tulvarees for ''Wanted by Cracken'' is a concept sketch by [[Ralph McQuarrie]] for the [[Mynock/Legends|mynocks]] of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="BTM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref><ref name="The Art of Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> An image derived from the concept sketch is used in both the [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded|second]]<ref name="AGTTSWU2 315">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> and [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded|third editions]] of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' to illustrate the entry on mynocks.<ref name="AGTTSWU3 372">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> The Tulvarees are mentioned in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which was published in [[2008]]; the book does not capitalize their name.<ref name="CSWE III 267" />

A mirrored version of the same concept image used for the Tulvarees appears in ''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]'', a book of art of aliens coupled with poems, jokes, and fiction written by [[Bob Carrau]] and published in 1993; the book does not provide a name for the species. The Tulvaree image accompanies a poem about a member of the species stalking, killing, and eating a victim before preying on another target.<ref name="MAGL">''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]''</ref> [[Lucasfilm]] employee [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the [[Holocron continuity database]], has indicated that information contained in the book is [[Canon|non-canonical]].<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=And is information from Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas canon? Unless referred to by a source that is in-continuity, the information is non-continuity.}}</ref>

As depicted in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'', the species was nocturnal and capable of surviving the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] of [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Each Tulvaree hunter planned surprise strikes in advance, choosing prey detached from a crowd and attacking when least expected with taunts as the hunter drew near—thus creating the most amount of fear in the victim. The Tulvaree then flew away with the prey and sometimes impaled it on an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]]. According to an original painting in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'', at least some Tulvarees possessed large, erect [[Ear/Legends|ears]], a prominent [[Nose/Legends|snout]], small [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] on the side of the head, and short [[Hair/Legends|fur]].<ref name="MAGL" />
